repaired_question_bank.question: How does batch normalization differ from layer normalization in the dimensions it normalizes over?
choices: Batch normalization normalizes each feature across examples in a batch, while layer normalization normalizes across features within an example.
Batch normalization only works on characters, while layer normalization only works on words.
Batch normalization removes nonlinearities, while layer normalization removes residual connections.
Batch normalization is a pooling operation, while layer normalization is a convolutional filter.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: Both methods rescale activations, but they compute their statistics over different axes. Batch normalization compares a feature across the batch, while layer normalization rescales features within an individual representation.

repaired_question_bank.question: Why can a size-1 convolution still be useful in an NLP convolutional model?
choices: It applies a learned position-wise transformation to each token representation without mixing information across the whole sentence.
It is identical to max pooling over the entire sentence.
It guarantees that the model uses character inputs rather than word inputs.
It can only copy the input representation unchanged.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: A width-1 convolution is like applying the same small feedforward layer at each position. It re-expresses each token vector while preserving the local, position-wise structure.
